anonymized_passage: when a schedule must be released for the next shift, before none in the scheduling model is known, a production scheduler chooses between an exact solver incumbent and a heuristic schedule using jobs, processing times, machine availability, costs, and the release deadline. The stated objective is to minimize tardiness and setup cost, subject to the condition that only solutions available before release can affect operations. Several operational considerations connect the decision to later outcomes, but their relationship is not specified. The decision is important and involves competing considerations that require further analysis. The scope of the resulting claim remains to be established. The study distinguishes mathematical solution quality from the value of having a usable schedule on time.
